name: canvas-faculty
description: Ask about YOUR OWN Cornell Canvas courses and students. Per-faculty, FERPA-scoped: every Canvas call is scoped to your personal Canvas token, so you only ever see your own courses. Powered by a Cornell-hosted n8n agent + the Cornell LiteLLM gateway.
long_description: A thin Cowork/MCP front-end over the program's 'Canvas: Faculty Agent' n8n webhook. The single tool `ask_canvas` sends your question to the webhook, forwarding your OWN Canvas API token as the X-Canvas-Token header (per-user scoping — under FERPA, one faculty member must never see another's student data) plus a shared cohort gate key as the X-Gate-Key header (the webhook validates this and returns 403 if it is missing or wrong). The agent runs read-only Canvas tools (list courses, list/search students, overlap, grade trajectory, assignment analytics) entirely on Cornell infrastructure. No Canvas data or LLM keys leave Cornell infra; your token and the gate key live ONLY in this plugin's local config (OS keychain).

canvas_webhook_url.title: Canvas faculty webhook URL
canvas_webhook_url.description: The n8n webhook for the Canvas faculty agent, e.g. https://n8n-dev.lcmain.aaii.cucloud.net/webhook/canvas-faculty

canvas_api_token.title: Your Canvas API token (X-Canvas-Token)
canvas_api_token.description: YOUR OWN Canvas API access token. Generate it in Canvas: Account -> Settings -> Approved Integrations -> New Access Token. This stays on your machine and scopes every query to your courses only. See the onboarding guide.

canvas_gate_key.title: Gate key (X-Gate-Key)
canvas_gate_key.description: Shared cohort gate secret the webhook requires before doing any work. Get it from the program administrator. Stored locally only.

tools.name: ask_canvas
tools.description: Ask about your own Canvas courses/students; returns a grounded answer scoped to your Canvas token.
